Radical Speed Month 2025 — Automattic

Static
into
WordPress.

Static Site Importer converts any custom HTML/CSS into a fully editable WordPress block theme—giving Studio a fast lane for site generation and giving customers a real path into WordPress from existing work.

Command

The Context

Radical Speed Month
is about real bets.

RSM gives Automattic engineers a dedicated month to build something that matters—fast. No committees, no roadmap review, just a clear problem and the space to solve it properly.

Static Site Importer is that bet: a single-command conversion layer that makes HTML/CSS a first-class input into the WordPress ecosystem. It changes the unit of work for Studio agents from "assemble blocks from scratch" to "build a site, then import it."

That shift has compounding effects across agent quality, customer journeys, and the broader conversation about where WordPress fits in a world increasingly comfortable with static-first tools.

  • Month-long focused build, zero committee overhead
  • Solves a real gap in Studio's site generation pipeline
  • Opens a credible data liberation path for existing HTML
  • Positions WordPress as the endpoint for the static-site trend
  • Ships as a reusable WP-CLI command, not a one-off script
  • Demo-able, measurable, and production-ready by end of month
Problem & Opportunity

The static-site
trend isn't slowing down.

The Problem

WordPress isn't the obvious answer anymore

Developers and agencies increasingly reach for static sites: they feel fast, portable, version-controllable, and trivially easy to generate with AI. When someone asks Claude or ChatGPT to "build a site," the answer arrives as clean HTML and CSS—not a block editor JSON blob.

Studio's site generation has felt this pressure. Generating block-format output directly requires careful prompt engineering, produces fragile markup, and leaves little room for creative layouts that don't conform to Gutenberg conventions. The debugging surface area is large and the output quality is inconsistent.

Meanwhile, customers arrive with existing assets—HTML exports, agency templates, generated prototypes—and face a hard choice: start over in WordPress, or stay outside it.

The Opportunity

Static HTML is the perfect intermediate format

AI agents are remarkably good at generating clean, semantic HTML and CSS. It's a well-understood format with decades of training data, instant browser validation, and zero proprietary conventions. The limiting factor has never been generating the HTML—it's been converting it into something WordPress can own.

Static Site Importer closes that gap. By treating HTML as the canonical output format for site generation—and handling the WordPress conversion as a reliable, deterministic step—Studio can produce better sites faster, support more diverse layouts, and give existing HTML assets a first-class path into WordPress.

The trend toward static doesn't have to route around WordPress. With this project, it routes through it.

How It Works

Four steps from
HTML to editable.

01

Write or provide HTML

Start with any static HTML document—agent-generated, hand-built, exported from a tool, or an existing customer template. No special conventions required. Pure HTML and CSS.

02

Run the importer

One WP-CLI command parses the HTML, extracts the CSS, identifies the document structure, and scaffolds a complete block theme with a front page template and associated styles.

03

Theme activates automatically

The generated theme is registered, activated, and set as the front page. The site immediately reflects the imported design. No manual file editing, no theme activation steps.

04

Edit in Site Editor

The customer lands in a fully functional WordPress site with the visual design they started with, ready to edit content in the Site Editor. No hand-off friction, no "this is approximate" disclaimer.

The Command

One line.
That's the interface.

Input — your static HTML
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <title>My Site</title>
  <style>
    /* Any CSS you want */
    body { font-family: 'Syne', sans-serif; }
    .hero { background: #c8ff00; padding: 120px; }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <header class="hero">
    <h1>Custom Design</h1>
    <p>Exactly as built.</p>
  </header>
  <!-- ... rest of site ... -->
</body>
</html>
Command — one WP-CLI call
# Import and activate in one step
wp static-site-importer import-theme \
  /path/to/index.html \
  --slug=my-custom-theme \
  --name="My Custom Theme" \
  --activate \
  --overwrite

# Output
Theme generated: my-custom-theme
Front page template created.
Theme activated successfully.
Done.
Studio integration — agent call
# Studio Code calls this after
# writing the static HTML
wp_cli(
  "static-site-importer import-theme"
  "/wordpress/tmp/static-site/index.html"
  "--slug=generated-theme"
  "--activate --overwrite"
)
Who It Helps

Every customer
with existing HTML.

Studio Agent

AI-generated sites, no block gymnastics

Agents write clean HTML and CSS—they're excellent at it. The importer handles the block conversion, so agents never need to learn Gutenberg's format. Better layouts, fewer retries, zero block validation errors.

Developer

Prototype to production without starting over

Built a quick HTML prototype to show a client? Run the importer. The prototype becomes a real WordPress site with editable content. The design the client approved is the design they get.

Agency

Deliver custom templates as WordPress themes

Agencies with existing HTML template libraries can import them directly. Each template becomes an activatable WordPress block theme. No manual block re-authoring. Client handoff via Site Editor.

Migrating Customer

HTML export gets a real WordPress home

Customers leaving Squarespace, Wix, or any static host often have HTML exports. Instead of starting from scratch in WordPress, they import what they have and edit from there.

Content Creator

Figma-to-WordPress without the middle steps

Tools that export Figma designs to HTML gain a clear path to WordPress. The gap between design tool output and live site narrows to a single command.

Enterprise

Brand templates as reusable site foundations

Enterprises with brand-approved HTML templates can deploy them as WordPress themes across multiple sites. Consistent design, centralized updates, maintainable in the block editor.

Studio Value

What this changes
for Studio.

  • 01

    Better one-shot site generation

    Agents generate HTML instead of block JSON. HTML is easier to generate well, easier to debug, and produces more reliable visual results. One-shot quality improves significantly.

  • 02

    Fewer fragile block-format prompts

    Block format prompts are brittle, require constant maintenance, and produce inconsistent output. With the importer, that whole class of problem disappears from the agent's surface area.

  • 03

    Easier debugging and iteration

    HTML renders directly in a browser. Agents can check their work, catch visual issues, and iterate without running a full WordPress installation. Faster development loop, fewer surprises.

  • 04

    One reusable conversion pipeline

    Every Studio agent and workflow shares the same reliable importer. Improvements to the conversion logic benefit everything at once instead of being duplicated across multiple prompt strategies.

  • 05

    Customer-provided templates

    A customer who arrives with an existing HTML template can hand it to Studio and end up with a working WordPress site. That conversion flow doesn't exist anywhere else in the ecosystem.

  • 06

    Cleaner agent-to-editor handoff

    The customer receives a site that looks exactly like what the agent built—and can edit it. No "this is approximate" moment. The handoff from agent output to human editor is seamless.

“
The best version of Studio isn't one where agents try to speak Gutenberg. It's one where agents speak HTML—which they already do well—and a reliable converter handles the rest. The importer makes that architecture real.
Static Site Importer — RSM 2025
Data Liberation

Existing HTML
deserves a home.

Millions of sites exist outside WordPress as static HTML—built by hand, generated by tools, exported by platforms, or assembled by AI. These aren't broken assets waiting to be discarded. They represent real design work, real content, and real customer investment.

WordPress's data liberation mission is about preserving that investment and making WordPress the natural next step, not a complete restart. Static Site Importer is a concrete implementation of that principle: your HTML comes in, a real WordPress site comes out, and you keep what you built.

The shift matters most at the edges of the ecosystem—the customer who generated a site prototype with an AI tool, the developer who has a clean template and doesn't want to re-learn the block editor from scratch, the agency that built in HTML because they always have.

For all of them, the importer says: your format is valid. We'll meet you where you are.

AI-generated HTML prototype
Squarespace / Wix HTML export
Hand-built custom template
Figma export / design tool output
Agency HTML template library
Legacy static site backup
↓
WordPress block theme — Site Editor ready

HTML in.
WordPress out.

Static Site Importer closes the loop between AI-generated output and editable WordPress sites. One reliable pipeline. Better generation. Real data liberation.
